title: lophophora lutea
intro: Lophophora lutea, also known as the gold barrel cactus or yellow peyote, is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to produce mescaline, a psychoactive alkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Lophophora lutea include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve mood, and even aid in the treatment of addiction and insomnia. In herbal preparations, the plant's mescaline is typically extracted and used in teas, tinctures, or capsules, although its use is highly regulated and subject to local laws and regulations.
